reason: The Hebrews are mentioned in the text fragment when Jonah identifies himself as an Hebrew to the men on the ship. However, the Hebrews are not depicted as captives in a foreign land tied to it. In this passage, Jonah is a Hebrew prophet sent to deliver a message to the city of Nineveh. While Jonah tries to flee to Tarshish, he ultimately fulfills his mission in Nineveh.
